jokes.text: Tom claiming to be a good friend for throwing the party while immediately revealing he's using it to 'stock the club with every available hottie'
jokes.setup: Tom presents himself as a selfless friend throwing a party
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's actually using it as a personal dating scheme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - shows his self-serving nature disguised as generosity. Well-constructed irony.

jokes.text: Tom's confidence that he'll have 'between one and four new girlfriends' by the end of the night
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate party planning scheme
jokes.punchline: The weirdly specific and overly optimistic range of potential girlfriends
jokes.explanation: The specificity of '1 to 4' girlfriends is perfectly absurd and reveals Tom's delusional confidence.

jokes.text: Andy guessing 'April' when asked about a song named after a month, and the awkward 'That would have been way better' response
jokes.setup: Andy wrote a song named after a month for April's birthday
jokes.punchline: April correctly guesses it's named April, making Andy realize his song title is worse
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy bumbling - well-intentioned but poorly executed gesture creates natural awkwardness.

jokes.text: April's deadpan confessional about being 21 and therefore 'the age that pretty much everyone agrees makes you an adult'
jokes.setup: April just turned 21
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact delivery about adulthood while clearly being immature
jokes.explanation: Perfect April delivery - the contrast between her deadpan wisdom and actual immaturity is spot-on.

jokes.text: Leslie's theatrical villain laugh that 'sounded like a chimp there at the end'
jokes.setup: Leslie doing an over-the-top evil laugh about her 'master plan'
jokes.punchline: Ron's dry observation that she sounded like a chimp
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - Leslie's enthusiasm deflated by Ron's perfectly timed deadpan observation.

jokes.text: The bureaucratic confusion about 'indefinitely' - 'Until when?' 'Indefinitely.' 'And when will that end?' 'Later than now.'
jokes.setup: Official announces indefinite postponement
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of explaining when indefinite ends
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic double-talk that perfectly captures government inefficiency through wordplay.

jokes.text: Leslie's defiant 'I'm not gonna fight them. Except that I am!' followed by Ron's resigned 'Okay.'
jokes.setup: Leslie seemingly accepting she can't fight the auditors
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal plus Ron's weary acceptance of Leslie's nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie impulsiveness paired with Ron's long-suffering patience with her antics.

jokes.text: Leslie's anger management technique: 'count backwards from 1,000 by sevens and think of warm brownies'
jokes.setup: Ron asking what Leslie does when angry
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and childish anger management technique
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and absurd anger management technique that feels both childish and somehow believable for Leslie.

jokes.text: Tom and Andy's terrible math trying to calculate the dating age rule, both arriving at different wrong answers
jokes.setup: Tom explaining the 'half your age plus seven' rule
jokes.punchline: Both characters failing basic math and confidently agreeing on wrong answers
jokes.explanation: Great showcase of both characters' intellectual limitations, made funnier by their mutual confidence.

jokes.text: Andy's immediate realization that 'Tom says it's okay' probably means 'it isn't okay'
jokes.setup: Andy asking Tom for dating advice
jokes.punchline: Andy's rare moment of wisdom recognizing Tom's terrible judgment
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Andy showing self-awareness about his friends' flaws, perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Ann explaining to Mark that couples are 'supposed to fight' and 'supposed to miss each other even if they're only gone for 20 minutes'
jokes.setup: Mark thinking their lack of fighting was good
jokes.punchline: Ann's counterintuitive relationship wisdom about healthy conflict
jokes.explanation: Solid relationship observation but fairly conventional wisdom, competently delivered.

jokes.text: Mark's response: 'I should have yelled at you way more'
jokes.setup: Ann's explanation about needing conflict in relationships
jokes.punchline: Mark's literal interpretation of needing to fight more
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective response, shows Mark's literal-mindedness in a relationship context.

jokes.text: Jerry's panicked 'Death!' response to seeing the state auditors
jokes.setup: State auditors arriving at the office
jokes.punchline: Jerry's immediate terror and dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - maximum fear expressed with minimal words, great economy.

jokes.text: Chris's over-the-top enthusiasm: 'there is quite literally nothing I would rather have in the world than a tour of the parks and recreation department'
jokes.setup: Being offered a simple office tour
jokes.punchline: Chris treating a mundane tour as the greatest possible experience
jokes.explanation: Establishes Chris's character perfectly - his extreme positivity about mundane things is both charming and absurd.

jokes.text: Leslie comparing eye contact with Chris to 'staring into the sun'
jokes.setup: Leslie describing her first impression of Chris
jokes.punchline: The metaphor of his positivity being blinding
jokes.explanation: Nice metaphor that captures Chris's overwhelming positivity in a relatable way.

jokes.text: Chris's carousel metaphor for government reform ending with 'get those happy kids back up on the horses where they belong!'
jokes.setup: Chris explaining their mission to fix the government
jokes.punchline: The childish carnival metaphor for serious budget cuts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that shows Chris's ability to make harsh realities sound cheerful and childlike.

jokes.text: Ron's enthusiastic response to budget cuts: 'Where do I start? What exactly will you be cutting? And how much of it, And can I watch you doing it while eating pork cracklins?'
jokes.setup: Ben asking about waste in the department
jokes.punchline: Ron's gleeful anticipation of government downsizing while eating pork cracklins
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - his libertarian joy at government cuts combined with the specific detail of pork cracklins is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Leslie's over-the-top defense of Jerry: 'He is one of the best people on the planet. He's universally adored here. If you fired him, there would be a revolt.'
jokes.setup: Ben asking about Jerry's value to the department
jokes.punchline: Leslie's absurd overstatement about the universally mocked Jerry
jokes.explanation: Great irony given Jerry's actual status in the office, shows Leslie's protective nature even for the least valued employee.

jokes.text: Ben's blunt correction of Chris's optimism: 'he said that because that sounds a lot better than, we're going to gut it with a machete'
jokes.setup: Leslie confused by the disconnect between Chris's optimism and reality
jokes.punchline: Ben's brutally honest metaphor for their actual mission
jokes.explanation: Perfectly establishes Ben's character as the realistic counterpart to Chris's optimism, great violent metaphor.

jokes.text: Leslie's sincere belief that the building might have feelings: 'There's a lot of history in this one, maybe it does.'
jokes.setup: Ben sarcastically asking if the building has feelings
jokes.punchline: Leslie's earnest consideration that it might
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her romantic idealism about everything, even inanimate objects, creates sweet absurdity.

jokes.text: Andy asking 'What's a not-gay way to ask him to go camping with me?' (presumably about Ben)
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to bond with the new guy
jokes.punchline: His awkward concern about the perceived implications of a camping invitation
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy social awkwardness - his innocent desire to make friends complicated by overthinking masculine dynamics.

jokes.text: April's response to being offered a birthday shot: 'now that it's legal, I've kind of lost interest'
jokes.setup: Being offered alcohol on her 21st birthday
jokes.punchline: The contrarian logic that legality makes it less appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - rebellious nature means losing interest once something becomes legal/sanctioned.

jokes.text: Tom bragging about his role in getting Trish crowned Miss Pawnee, with her talent being 'looking amazing'
jokes.setup: Tom trying to impress women with his connections
jokes.punchline: The shallow 'talent' reveals the meaninglessness of his accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom shallow bragging, competently executed but not particularly original.

jokes.text: The sound effect and visual of someone apparently falling or getting hurt during the party
jokes.setup: Party atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Someone getting hurt, indicated by the 'Oh!' and reaction
jokes.explanation: Generic physical comedy moment, adds to party chaos but lacks specificity or character.

jokes.text: Andy's enthusiastic greeting contrasted with Ann asking if she was a good girlfriend
jokes.setup: Former couple running into each other at the party
jokes.punchline: Ann's need for validation from her ex while drunk
jokes.explanation: Realistic drunk behavior creating natural awkwardness between exes, well-observed character moment.

jokes.text: Ann's drunk question: 'were there times that you were like, if I don't see Ann in, like, ten seconds, I'm gonna die?'
jokes.setup: Ann seeking validation about their past relationship
jokes.punchline: The overly specific and needy nature of her question
jokes.explanation: Good drunk dialogue that reveals Ann's insecurity while being believably awkward.

jokes.text: Ann's bitter realization: 'I dated him for three years. Now he's an adult with a job. And some other girl is gonna reap the rewards of my hard work? That's bull[bleep].'
jokes.setup: Ann reflecting on her relationship with Andy
jokes.punchline: Treating relationship growth like an investment with unfair returns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about relationships as investment/labor that many can relate to, perfectly delivered with righteous anger.

jokes.text: Tom's 'classic game. Plant the seed. Harvest like a half hour later' while giving a bottlecap as a 'romantic' gesture
jokes.setup: Tom trying to create a romantic moment with a bottlecap
jokes.punchline: His cynical calculation about the timing of his seduction attempt
jokes.explanation: Great Tom moment showing his pickup artistry mixed with terrible timing and cheap romantic gestures.

jokes.text: Jean-Ralphio's story about waiting outside the wrong woman's house for five days and still getting to 'third base, over the pants'
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio explaining his persistence in dating
jokes.punchline: The wrong house detail and oddly specific sexual achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jean-Ralphio story - combines his delusional confidence with absurd details and inappropriate oversharing.

jokes.text: Andy's confused realization: 'I thought she liked me. I guess I'm super bad at picking up signals'
jokes.setup: April clearly showing interest in Andy all episode
jokes.punchline: Andy's complete obliviousness to obvious romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy obliviousness - his understatement about being bad at signals when he missed all of April's interest.

jokes.text: Andy calling Jean-Ralphio 'that Ralph Macchio guy'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to remember Jean-Ralphio's name
jokes.punchline: Confusing him with the Karate Kid actor
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy name confusion, reasonably funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Tom frantically asking multiple people 'Who the [bleep] did I give a bottlecap to?' having forgotten his own romantic gesture
jokes.setup: Tom's earlier bottlecap seduction strategy
jokes.punchline: Complete failure to track his own pickup attempts
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing Tom's strategies backfiring spectacularly, perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: April drunkenly mixing up 'thirsty' and 'weird': 'You know what's thirsty? You know what's weird? How thirsty I get when I'm weird. When I'm drunk.'
jokes.setup: April trying to explain her drunken state
jokes.punchline: The confused word substitution revealing how drunk she is
jokes.explanation: Good drunk dialogue that feels authentic while maintaining April's character voice.

jokes.text: Leslie's drunk hostility toward Ben: 'save your breath, okay? Just get out of here. Because this is a party with my friends And you're trying to fire all my friends.'
jokes.setup: Ben trying to be friendly at the party
jokes.punchline: Leslie's blunt drunk honesty about why she dislikes him
jokes.explanation: Realistic drunk confrontation that advances character relationships, competently executed.

jokes.text: Ann sarcastically praising Leslie: 'Leslie, that was so professional. I'm so proud of you.'
jokes.setup: Leslie's completely unprofessional behavior toward Ben
jokes.punchline: Ann's deadpan sarcastic praise
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sarcasm, good character moment for Ann as the voice of reason.

jokes.text: Ann's hangover crisis: 'I think I may have made out with someone last night' and her complete inability to remember who
jokes.setup: Ann waking up with a hangover
jokes.punchline: The classic drunk embarrassment of not remembering romantic encounters
jokes.explanation: Standard hangover comedy, relatable but not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: Chris's vitamin routine: 'B12? Evening primrose oil? Willow bark? Magnesium?' and his belief he'll live 150 years
jokes.setup: Chris offering hangover remedies
jokes.punchline: The extensive supplement list and his confident longevity prediction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character establishment - his extreme health consciousness taken to absurd logical conclusion.

jokes.text: Leslie's defiant speech: 'You may hold my fate in your hands like a small bird, But I still think you're an ass.'
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to apologize to Ben
jokes.punchline: The poetic metaphor followed by blunt insult
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - her tendency toward poetic metaphors undermined by her inability to actually be diplomatic.

jokes.text: Chris needing someone present when he takes his multivitamin because of 'choking hazard'
jokes.setup: Chris's extreme health consciousness
jokes.punchline: Being afraid of his own vitamins despite his health obsession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction in Chris's character - health fanatic afraid of vitamins, perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: April's relief contrasted with Andy's observation: 'For once, it's Ann who blacked out drunk And not Andy.'
jokes.setup: Ann having a blackout drunk experience
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact observation about the role reversal
jokes.explanation: Good character observation that establishes Andy's usual behavior while noting the unusual situation.

jokes.text: Tom's bar tab revelation: '47 drinks' including 'Ten cosmos, eight Smirnoff ices, And everything else starts with the word pomegranate'
jokes.setup: Tom checking his bar tab from the party
jokes.punchline: The absurd number and specific feminine drink choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - his pretentious drink choices and the absurd quantity reveal his failed strategy.

jokes.text: Tom's mathematical confusion about his imaginary orgy: 'The problem is I only have 15 penises. So there would have been 28 girls there that were really upset with me.'
jokes.setup: Tom fantasizing about a 43-way
jokes.punchline: The impossible math and his concern about disappointing women
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Tom logic - the impossible anatomy combined with his genuine concern about customer satisfaction is comedy gold.

jokes.text: Ben's backstory reveal: 'When I was 18, I ran for Mayor of my small town and won' followed by 'Here's the thing, though, about 18-year-olds. They're idiots.'
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his political background
jokes.punchline: His self-aware assessment of teenage wisdom
jokes.explanation: Great character development moment that explains Ben's competence while showing self-awareness about youthful mistakes.

jokes.text: Ben's admission that his parents grounded him after being impeached as mayor
jokes.setup: Ben describing the consequences of his political failure
jokes.punchline: The juvenile punishment for a failed political career
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd detail that humanizes Ben while showing the surreal nature of being an impeached teenage mayor.

jokes.text: The revelation that Ben played 'Whoomp! There it is' at his mayoral swearing-in ceremony
jokes.setup: Discussion of Ben's teenage political career
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate song choice for a formal ceremony
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of teenage poor judgment, the specific song choice makes it memorably cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: Ann methodically asking every man she knows 'did we make out last night?' including the horrified Ron
jokes.setup: Ann trying to solve her blackout mystery
jokes.punchline: Ron's disgusted 'Good God, woman, no' reaction
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Ann's desperation and perfect Ron reaction - his horror at the suggestion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: The revelation that Ann was so drunk she gave Ben her phone instead of her phone number
jokes.setup: Ann trying to figure out how Ben can contact her
jokes.punchline: The absurd drunk logic of giving away her actual phone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant drunk logic moment that perfectly captures the confusion of blackout behavior.

jokes.text: Leslie hearing 'really loud circus music' when told the government will be shut down
jokes.setup: Chris delivering devastating news about government shutdown
jokes.punchline: Leslie's mental breakdown manifesting as circus music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie mental breakdown moment - the specific detail of circus music captures the surreal horror of bureaucratic disaster.

jokes.text: Andy's song lyrics: 'November, your bangs are cute / November, your voice is a flute' followed by the reveal 'That song is about April'
jokes.setup: Andy's earlier promise to write April a birthday song
jokes.punchline: The terrible lyrics and wrong month reveal his complete incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that delivers on the song promise while showcasing Andy's sweet incompetence - wrong month, terrible lyrics, but genuine effort.
